process_interval.description: Represents a time interval, encoded as a Timestamp start (inclusive) and a Timestamp end (exclusive). The start must be less than or equal to the end. When the start equals the end, the interval is empty (matches no time). When both start and end are unspecified, the interval matches any time. Structure is [documented below](#nested_process_interval).

location.description: The location of the resource. This is the geographical region where the Chronicle instance resides, such as "us" or "europe-west2".

instance.description: The unique identifier for the Chronicle instance, which is the same as the customer ID.

rule.description: The Rule ID of the rule.

retrohunt.description: The retrohunt ID of the Retrohunt. A retrohunt is an execution of a Rule over a time range in the past.

project.description: If it is not provided, the provider project is used.
